title: Grona dalzellii
intro: Grona dalzellii, also known as Indian staff vine, is a climbing vine native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Grona dalzellii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Grona dalzellii is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Grona dalzellii is a plant species, and its classification is within the family Rutaceae. Grona dalzellii is a genus of flowering plants that is endemic to the Western Ghats of India.
